info.title: onari_ml
info.description:
# onari_ml:dev

[![pypi_status](https://img.shields.io/badge/BentoML-1.4.31-informational)](https://pypi.org/project/BentoML)
[![documentation_status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/bentoml/badge/?version=latest)](https://docs.bentoml.com/)
[![join_slack](https://badgen.net/badge/Join/BentoML%20Slack/cyan?icon=slack)](https://l.bentoml.com/join-slack-swagger)
[![BentoML GitHub Repo](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/bentoml/bentoml?style=social)](https://github.com/bentoml/BentoML)
[![Twitter Follow](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/bentomlai?label=Follow%20BentoML&style=social)](https://twitter.com/bentomlai)

This is a Machine Learning Service created with BentoML.

## Help

* [📖 Documentation](https://docs.bentoml.com/en/latest/): Learn how to use BentoML.
* [💬 Community](https://l.bentoml.com/join-slack-swagger): Join the BentoML Slack community.
* [🐛 GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bentoml/BentoML/issues): Report bugs and feature requests.
* Tip: you can also [customize this README](https://docs.bentoml.com/en/latest/reference/bentoml/bento-build-options.html#description).

get.description: Health check endpoint. Expecting an empty response with status code 200 when the service is in health state. The /healthz endpoint is deprecated. (since Kubernetes v1.16)
Health check endpoint for Kubernetes. Healthy endpoint responses with a 200 OK status.
A 200 OK status from /readyz endpoint indicated the service is ready to accept traffic. From that point and onward, Kubernetes will use /livez endpoint to perform periodic health checks.
Prometheus metrics endpoint. The /metrics responses with a 200. The output can then be used by a Prometheus sidecar to scrape the metrics of the service.

post.description:
Delete capture data from Neo4j (Pipeline Direct) and Graphiti (EpisodicNodes).

        Called fire-and-forget from delete-capture Edge Function.
        After cleanup, updates capture_deletion_log on Supabase via REST.

        Schema 1 — Pipeline Direct (HTTP Transaction API):
          Deletes Capture node + all MENTIONS relationships.
          Entity nodes are shared across captures and remain.

        Schema 2 — Graphiti (Neo4j Driver via Graphiti Client):
          Deletes EpisodicNodes whose name starts with 'capture-{id}'.
          Covers single-speaker, diarized-self, and diarized-other variants.

Train HDBSCAN model on user's segment embeddings.

        Uses prediction_data=True to enable approximate_predict() for new segments.
        Applies UMAP dimension reduction (1024D -> 5D) before clustering.

        Args:
            user_id: User identifier for model caching
            embeddings: List of 1024D BGE-M3 embeddings
            segment_ids: Corresponding segment IDs
            min_cluster_size: Optional override (default: dynamic based on data size)

        Returns:
            labels: Cluster assignments (-1 = outlier)
            cluster_count: Number of clusters formed
            outlier_rate: Percentage of outliers

Predict cluster assignments for new segments using approximate_predict.

        O(k log n) instead of O(n^2) - much faster for incremental clustering.
        Requires cluster_fit to have been called first for this user.

        Args:
            user_id: User identifier (for cached model)
            embeddings: New segment embeddings (1024D)
            segment_ids: Corresponding segment IDs

        Returns:
            labels: Predicted cluster assignments
            strengths: Confidence scores for assignments

Check if user's model should be retrained.

        Per CYCLE8 research: retrain when pending_points > 20% of training data.

        Args:
            user_id: User identifier
            pending_count: Number of segments added since last training
            total_count: Total segments in training set

        Returns:
            should_retrain: Boolean recommendation
            reason: Explanation

Speaker diarization endpoint using pyannote speaker-diarization-3.1.

        Input: audio_url (signed URL), min_speakers, max_speakers
        Output: segments [{start, end, speaker}], speaker_count,
        segment_embeddings, processing_time_ms.

        Speaker embeddings are returned as one optional 256-dim list per segment.
        The worker stores them in speaker_segments.embedding so the user-visible
        voice-profile confirmation path can match known speakers. Audio remains
        ephemeral in this endpoint.
Generate embeddings using BGE-M3

Phase 3: Voice enrollment utterance processing.

        Accepts a single utterance recording for voice profile enrollment.
        Extracts a 256-dim WeSpeaker embedding and runs Level 2A + 2B anti-spoofing
        checks. Returns the embedding if accepted so the Edge Function can aggregate
        embeddings and write them to voice_profiles after 10 accepted utterances.

        Architecture: Pure ML compute endpoint — no Supabase writes.
        DB persistence is handled by the voice-profile-enroll Edge Function,
        which manages session state and finalizes the voice profile.

        GDPR: Audio is processed in-memory and from temp file only.
        Temp file is deleted in the finally block. No audio is persisted.
        Only the extracted embedding (256 floats) is returned.

        Input:
            audio_base64: base64-encoded M4A or WAV audio of the enrollment utterance
            enrollment_session_id: UUID of the enrollment_sessions row (for logging)
            utterance_index: 0-based index of the sentence in the session (for logging)
            response_latency_ms: client-measured time from sentence display to recording
                                 start (ms). Used for Level 2B anti-spoofing check.

        Output:
            passed: bool — True if both anti-spoofing checks passed AND duration >= 1.5s
            embedding: list[float] (256 values, L2-normalized) if passed, null if rejected
            rejection_reason: human-readable reason if rejected, null if passed
            anti_spoofing_checks: diagnostic dict with individual check results
            duration_s: detected speech duration in seconds

Add an episode to Graphiti temporal memory.

        This processes a capture/text through Graphiti's entity extraction,
        relationship building, and temporal tracking.

        IMPORTANT: Takes 5-15 seconds per episode (200 words average).

        Args:
            episode_name: Unique identifier (e.g., "capture-{uuid}")
            episode_body: The text content (transcript, resolved coreferences recommended)
            source_description: Context (e.g., "Voice capture from user {user_id}")
            reference_time: ISO timestamp (defaults to now if not provided)
            resolve_coreferences: If True, runs coreference resolution first
            group_id: User ID for multi-tenant isolation (recommended for production)

        Returns:
            episode_id: The created episode UUID
            entities_extracted: Number of entities found
            edges_created: Number of relationships created
            processing_time_ms: Time taken

Return Graphiti community metadata for one isolated user group.

        This is a read-only projection endpoint for the voice document
        synthesis layer. It does not expose raw episode text.

Return domain-tagged entities grouped for document synthesis.

        Preferred grouping follows code/document episode names
        (``py-*``, ``ts-*``, ``doc-*``). If a user's domain entities are not
        tied to such episode names, they are honestly grouped under
        ``_domain`` instead of fabricating module boundaries.

Retrieve specific nodes from Graphiti by their UUIDs.

        Useful for Parent-Child Retrieval pattern:
        1. Search returns segment/fact UUIDs
        2. Fetch full parent capture context

        Args:
            node_uuids: List of node UUIDs to retrieve

        Returns:
            nodes: List of node data

Search Graphiti temporal memory.

        Uses hybrid search (embeddings + graph traversal) to find relevant
        entities and facts from the user's captured thoughts.

        Args:
            query: Search query
            num_results: Max results to return (default: 10)
            center_node_uuid: Optional node to center search around
            group_ids: List of user IDs to filter results (recommended for production)

        Returns:
            results: List of matched entities/facts with scores

Get Graphiti statistics for a user (GAP 6: Pipeline Testing).

        Returns entity count, relationship count, and optionally recent entities.
        Uses Neo4j driver from Graphiti client for direct Cypher queries.

        Args:
            user_id: User identifier (group_id in Graphiti)
            include_recent_entities: Whether to include recent entity names
            recent_entity_limit: Max number of recent entities to return

        Returns:
            entity_count: Total entities for user
            relationship_count: Total relationships for user
            recent_entities: List of recent entity names (if requested)
Health check endpoint

Webhook endpoint for starting async pipeline processing.
        Called by start-processing Edge Function (thin dispatcher).

        Modes:
        - VOICE: audio_storage_path points to real audio file in Supabase Storage
        - TEXT: audio_storage_path='text-input', text_input contains the user's text

        Returns 202 immediately, processes in background thread.

Unified ML processing pipeline for captures.

        This endpoint combines multiple ML operations into a single call,
        reducing Edge Function HTTP calls from ~20 to ~3.

        Pipeline stages:
        1. Segmentation (SaT) - Split transcript into sentences
        2. Coreference Resolution (parallel) - Resolve pronouns per segment
        3. Embeddings (batch) - BGE-M3 for all segments + full transcript
        4. (removed) Emotion Detection — XLM-EMO removed in Phase 25

        Args:
            text: The transcript text to process
            user_id: User identifier for logging
            capture_id: Capture identifier for logging
            language: Optional language code (de/en), auto-detected if not provided

        Returns:
            segments: List of segment texts
            segment_embeddings: List of embeddings for each segment
            coreferences: List of coreference results per segment
            emotions: List of emotion results per segment
            full_embedding: Embedding for full transcript
            processing_time_ms: Total processing time
            partial_failures: List of non-critical failures (processing continues)

Resolve pronouns to their referents using LLM (Mistral).

        This replaces the coreferee library approach due to dependency conflicts.
        Uses Mistral for high-quality, multilingual coreference resolution.

        Args:
            text: The text with pronouns to resolve
            entities: Optional list of known entities to prioritize

        Returns:
            resolved_text: Text with pronouns replaced by their referents
            resolutions: List of pronoun -> entity mappings

Segment text into sentences using SaT (Segment any Text).

        Uses TED-style domain adaptation for speech patterns.
        Returns segments with soft boundary probabilities for later retrieval optimization.

        Args:
            text: The text to segment
            return_probabilities: If True, returns probability scores for each boundary
            language: Optional language code (de, en). If not provided, auto-detected.

        Returns:
            segments: List of text segments
            boundaries: List of boundary positions with probabilities (if return_probabilities=True)

Multi-scale semantic segmentation combining SaT sentence boundaries
        with LangChain SemanticChunker for topic detection.

        This provides:
        - Fine-grained: Sentence boundaries from SaT
        - Coarse-grained: Topic boundaries from SemanticChunker
        - Cross-segment connections: Similarity scores between segments

        Args:
            text: The text to segment
            buffer_size: Number of sentences to group for semantic comparison (default: 2)
            threshold_type: "percentile", "standard_deviation", or "gradient" (default)

        Returns:
            sentences: Fine-grained sentence segments from SaT
            topics: Coarse-grained topic segments from SemanticChunker
            cross_connections: Pairs of similar segments (similarity > 0.75)

Transcribe audio to text using Whisper large-v3.
        Accepts either base64-encoded audio or a URL to download from.
        URL mode eliminates 33% base64 overhead.

        When word_timestamps=True, also returns word-level timing data for
        speaker-word alignment (Path A diarization pipeline).
        Default behavior (word_timestamps=False) is unchanged.
